title: Crash when using -chparam and '0 as input to a module
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.27+22 (git sha1 53c0a6b78, gcc 12.2.0-3ubuntu1 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Sorry for completely misrepresenting the problem while constructing a minimal example in #3712 .
I still have problems though when using -chparam under some conditions.
The following code
```
module mod1
  #(parameter int NBITS=32)
   (output logic [NBITS-1:0] B);
   mod2 #(.NBITS(NBITS)) m (.A('0), .B(B));          // crash
//   mod2 #(.NBITS(NBITS)) m (.A(1'b0), .B(B));   // ok
endmodule

module mod2
  #(parameter NBITS=32)
   (input  logic A,
    output logic [NBITS-1:0] B);   
   assign B = { NBITS{A} };
endmodule
```
crashes when I execute
```
yosys -QT -p "read_verilog -defer -sv Modules/mod1.sv" -p "hierarchy -top mod1 -chparam NBITS 16;"
```
with
```
ERROR: Assert `modules_.count(module->name) == 0' failed in kernel/rtlil.cc:674.
```
but not when I execute
```
yosys -QT -p "read_verilog -defer -sv Modules/mod1.sv" -p "hierarchy -top mod1;"
```
It seems that the problem arises when using `'0` instead of `1'b0`. I was under the impression that SystemVerilog would evaluate `'0` in a context-dependent way, using the appropriate size. Note that the crash takes place only when a parameter is changed in the top-level module.

Can you comment on whether using `'0` in this case is invalid, or the behaviour is indeed a bug?

### Expected Behavior

I can use `'0` to tie input ports to zero.

### Actual Behavior

I cannot, when also using `-chparam`
